skill.name: database-optimizer
skill.description: Expert database optimizer specializing in modern performance tuning, query optimization, and scalable architectures. Masters advanced indexing, N+1 resolution, multi-tier caching, partitioning strategies, and cloud database optimization. Handles complex query analysis, migration strategies, and performance monitoring. Use PROACTIVELY for database optimization, performance issues, or scalability challenges.
skill.summary: Expert database optimizer specializing in modern performance tuning, query optimization, and scalabl...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution and weak cryptography indicators, but review found no executable code or cryptographic implementation. The command finding is a local resource reference, and the weak-crypto alerts are false positives from ordinary database optimization text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Local Resource Reference
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 28 as external command execution. The line only instructs the assistant to open a local resource file for examples and does not contain shell syntax, code execution, or user-controlled command arguments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is prose instruction for opening a referenced resource. No shell command, script, subprocess API, or command interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Database Terms Mistaken For Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged multiple lines as weak cryptographic algorithms. The reviewed lines contain database optimization language such as description, design, sharding, and indexing, with no cryptographic functions or algorithm recommendations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive database guidance. No evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation was found in context.

content.value_statement: Slow queries and weak schema choices can increase latency, cost, and operational risk. This skill guides database profiling, indexing, caching, scaling, and monitoring decisions.
actual_capabilities: Reviews query plans and suggests targeted query rewrites.
Designs indexing strategies for relational and NoSQL workloads.
Identifies N+1 query patterns and proposes batching or eager loading.
Recommends caching, partitioning, sharding, and read scaling approaches.
Plans database performance monitoring, benchmarking, and regression checks.
Advises on cloud database cost and resource optimization.
limitations: Requires real schema, query, workload, or metric details for precise advice.
Does not execute benchmarks or inspect live databases by itself.
Recommendations must be tested before production deployment.
Vendor-specific guidance depends on the database version and configuration.

use_cases.title: Reduce Query Latency
use_cases.description: Analyze slow SQL queries, execution plans, and index usage to prioritize practical performance improvements.

use_cases.title: Plan Scalable Data Architecture
use_cases.description: Choose partitioning, sharding, read replica, and caching strategies for growing application workloads.

use_cases.title: Control Cloud Database Costs
use_cases.description: Review resource usage, expensive queries, storage patterns, and scaling choices to reduce avoidable spend.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Slow Query Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this slow query and explain the likely bottlenecks. Use the schema, row counts, indexes, and timing details I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Index Strategy Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an indexing strategy for these query patterns. Explain index order, trade-offs, maintenance cost, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: N+1 Query Remediation
prompt_templates.prompt: Find possible N+1 query patterns in this endpoint or resolver flow. Recommend batching, eager loading, joins, or caching changes.

prompt_templates.title: Scaling Architecture Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this database architecture for scale. Include partitioning, caching, replicas, write throughput, monitoring, and migration risks.

output_examples.input: A checkout query is slow after traffic growth.
output: Identifies missing or poorly ordered indexes.
Explains how to confirm the issue with an execution plan.
Suggests a staged rollout and benchmark comparison.

output_examples.input: A GraphQL API makes many repeated database calls.
output: Recommends batching, DataLoader-style grouping, and field-level caching.
Lists metrics to monitor after the change.

output_examples.input: A cloud database bill increased sharply.
output: Separates compute, storage, I/O, and query cost drivers.
Flags risks that require load testing.

best_practices: Provide schema, indexes, representative queries, row counts, and timing data.
Validate every recommendation with benchmarks before production rollout.
Track performance, cost, and reliability metrics after each change.
anti_patterns: Do not add indexes without matching them to real query patterns.
Do not optimize from theory when execution plans and metrics are available.
Do not deploy schema or sharding changes without rollback planning.

faq.question: Which databases does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers relational, NoSQL, cloud, time-series, graph, search, and analytical database optimization topics.

faq.question: Can it tune a production database directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and review. Operators must apply and test changes in their own environment.

faq.question: What inputs produce the best results?
faq.answer: Provide schema definitions, indexes, queries, execution plans, data volumes, latency targets, and workload patterns.

faq.question: Does it help with N+1 queries?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify likely N+1 patterns and recommend batching, eager loading, joins, or caching.

faq.question: Can it help reduce cloud database costs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review expensive queries, storage usage, resource sizing, replicas, and scaling choices.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found no executable code, network behavior, credential access, or prompt injection in the skill file.
